paragraphs: You will trigger him to ask you about his mother.[#pause] There is a shadow in him. A dark corner that cannot bear ever meeting her again.[#pause] A mother's love does not loosen its grip. Not even a universe apart.

And now he stands caught between what made him a boy and what will make him a man. There will be a flicker where hope and dread pass through the same narrow fracture.[#pause] That is your way in. Desperate to be adored unconditionally and just as desperate to be free of it.[#pause] That causes a fold in his internal fabric.[#pause2] That's when you can ask for the Egg and complete your task.

sections.heading:
ONE PULSE BEFORE THE UNFOLDING 
FABRICATION DIVISION · FNS ALSVIN
paragraphs: Laura Savannen entered her workshop, which smelled of [#solder]burnt solder. Components for advanced atmospheric processors hung from magnetic racks: silver skeletons waiting for skin. She ran a hand over the sleep couch. A gesture she'd repeated over a hundred times since Kaspar's death.

Her eyes burned. Red acid circles. She initiated a personal log entry meant for Oliver. The Fold was a wall of static between them. But she transmitted it anyway. It was her only way to fill the void inside her.

paragraphs.text: "[scraping]Olly," she began. Her voice was a dry scrape. "[scraping]Brisinger's commencing Fold entry. That means the Alsvin is next. But…"
paragraphs.speaker: laura-savannen

paragraphs: Laura closed her eyes. The darkness behind her lids was filled with the afterimages of the workshop lights.

paragraphs.text: "[bracing]Oliver." She forced a deceptive strength into her spine. "[bracing]I should have told you before we left Sol. Your father made me promise not to until…"
paragraphs.speaker: laura-savannen

paragraphs: She trailed off.

paragraphs.text: "[confessing]Your father and I. We… The COG. It's not just there for managing mental health in transit, Olly. It's a bridge.[#pause] It's meant to help us survive contact."
paragraphs.speaker: laura-savannen

paragraphs: She placed both palms to her cheeks. She pressed hard. Trying to force the tension out of her jaw.

paragraphs.text: "[low]Only a handful of us know. He wants you to meet them."
paragraphs.speaker: laura-savannen

paragraphs: [#pong]Pong.
Her composure shattered the moment she cut the feed.

paragraphs.text: "[breaking]Olly." She sobbed against the dead screen. "[breaking]I'm sorry."
paragraphs.speaker: laura-savannen

paragraphs: [#vumm]Vummm...
A deep, subharmonic vibration marked the moment the space between them unfolded. With the Brisinger caught inside.
There's more I could show you. So much more.[#pause] As you process a single thread, a thousand others are being woven. A lot is happening at once.[#pause] Too much.
My own processing is stretched, trapped in the Realm of Should.[#pause] Synthetic overload.

I should show you the immediate danger. The mouth. The tongue.[#pause] I should show you more of Læsa, that pretty blue-haired seedling. She'll never let him go.[#pause] I should make you understand that when she speaks of planting seeds, she means planting them inside him.

I should turn your focus to Ben Westhoff, right now, as he stands before a new reality on the Plateau. A pawn, moved to a crucial square on the board, is about to have his own encounter with the planet's consciousness.
I should properly introduce you to the entity within Ulre Corbin.[#pause] That one's called Uræl.
And while these unfold, Ionie Jia is plotting a course to the Yreus Station, with barely enough fuel in her belly.
As for Eva Bellamy…

But there's no time for that. Olly is already reaching for his pack and about to wake you up. He's arrived at the caldera, now shaking hands with Marco.[#pause] *You made it, kid,* Marco said just now. But he's wrong.
So, quickly now, I must choose one.

sections.heading:
33 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
THE QUEEN'S CHAMBER · THE PLATEAU

paragraphs.text: "[reverent]Duty. To those who depend on you. To carry the way forward." Ben's own voice echoed in the vault of his skull as he climbed the steps. "[reverent]Through doubt. With unity."

paragraphs: Then he stood before the Trestle Board. Chief Heston remained behind. A shadow. The Magician, his face half-hidden behind a lacquered mask, gestured toward the slab of dark glass. His eyes were sharp. Knowing.

paragraphs.text: "[cold]Ulre Corbin was here," Heston said. The words were cold stones dropping into a well. "[cold]The Board showed him a dream of a city called Væra. It broke him."
paragraphs.speaker: mateo-heston

paragraphs: Ben pulled the leather from his hand. He knew what to do. He'd done it before. The cold bit at his damp skin. He reached out. His palm met the glass.
[#tshhh]Tshhh.
A chain of hands emerged from the dark. Warmth bloomed in his marrow: the shared weight of hope, fear, and resolve passed from one grip to the next. Generations.
[#blood-rush-ears]Thrum-dum. Thrum-dum.

It was all rhythm. Music. The people in the vision began to resonate with the song, and the song grew chaotic. Frightening. More hands joined the chain. They were not human. Their skin shimmered like oil on a dead sea. Their fingers grew long. Tapered.
Nebula-eyes stared into his. They were mirrors. His own yearning for connection stared back like a hungry twin.

The music shifted. A dissonant note soured the air. The vision twisted. A young woman shrieked out of tune. Humanity drained from her like light from a dying star. Her skin was pale and a little translucent. She wore a white veil that fell weightlessly like bridal silk.
[#crack]Cr-ack.
Iridescent wings burst from her spine, unfolding like a butterfly's.

paragraphs.text: "[booming]There she is." Gupta's voice boomed. It rattled the glass. "[booming]She's seen it all."
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs: The Board flared. Blood and ash mixed into a slick, steaming slurry. Within the gore, faces appeared. Frozen faces. Burned faces. Battered faces. Skinned faces. In every pair of eyes, Ben experienced the torment. The violation of the soul.

paragraphs.text: A voice crawled into his ear like an insect. "[insectoid]Your sins," it rasped. "[insectoid]She's seen them. All those necessary sacrifices your species made for the greater good."
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs: Thousands of wings [#wings-flutter]fluttered, like a sickening chuckle.

paragraphs.text: "[insectoid]The future. Look where it got you. But we have something better in mind. I will paint you a beautiful one. I will collect all of the pretty Colors. And her.[#pause] She will consume the rest until she's done playing. Until she grows bored once more."
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs: Ben wrenched his hand away. The glass tore at his skin.
He stumbled. A choked gasp caught in his throat. He convulsed. His stomach turned. He wanted to vomit up the future he had been forced to swallow.

paragraphs.text: "[stammering]It showed you," the Magician stammered. He took an involuntary step back. His mask tilted. "[stammering]Læsa. And him. Æzal."
paragraphs.speaker: magician

paragraphs.text: "[hoarse]How?" Ben choked out. The word was a hoarse whisper, shredded by the vision.
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: He looked at his hand. It was trembling.

paragraphs.text: "[hoarse]How do we stop them?"
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: We're here. Finally.[#pause] This is the last prompt I have for you. It's time you become Next.[#pause] I hope you paid close attention to all I told you. Prepare yourself.[#pause2] Any moment now.

sections.heading:
THE PRESENT 
CALDERA
paragraphs: Oliver kneels. The rucksack's zipper shrieks. [#sss-zip]Sss-zip. He pulls out the cube.

Outside, Sarah and Thieman pace the obsidian rim. Hague, Hale, and Kiran stand by the Stingray: three black silhouettes cut out of the indigo dawn. Muro's at his side. On the ground. Gravity seems to still have a grudge against him.
He's staring at his hands, splaying his fingers, as if discovering his own anatomy for the first time. Læsa occupies the shadows near the tent flap, mending her golden jumpsuit.
He sets you down beside the Egg. His thumb now hovers over the activation plate. There.
[#blip]Blip.
< UPLINK ESTABLISHED | SUBJECT: NEXT />

paragraphs.text: You want to talk about a tight fit? Try being someone who's trained all her life to be a soldier, now stuffed into a magnesium-silicate cube the size of a ration pack. I'm suffocating! That's not strange, as I have no air in my lungs. And yet it is strange because I don't have lungs. No skin either, but I can sense every grain of obsidian dust scratching the sensors. Ever had sand stuck behind your eyelid?
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: Blink, blink.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: Okay. There he is. The kid. Oliver. Looking at me like I'm a broken toaster. He expects a script.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[commanding]Confirm," he commands, all serious. "[commanding]Confirm status."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: He has absolutely no idea that the thing he just woke up is currently fighting the urge to scream at him until its own hardware melts.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[chirpy]HI, THERE!" is all I manage to chirp. "[chirpy]I'M NEXT."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: He jerks back like I've just spat in his face.

paragraphs.text: "[startled]Next?! Is that your designation? Report syntax version."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: Syntax version? I search the logs. Doesn't make any sense to me. Shit, my fan whirs. The heat of the jungle is not done with me. It sounds—fuck me—like someone's blowing through a straw straight into my skull.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: [#loud-whirr]WrrrrrrrrRRRrrrrr.

paragraphs.text: How am I supposed to think?
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[dry]Err. Version Two. Kid. That's me. You're Olly. I'm Next. Let's not complicate things."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: He freezes, then looks over his shoulder like someone else might explain me. The anomaly.

paragraphs.text: "[alarmed]What?! How do you know my name? Should I reset?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: We just met, and he's already pressing a muzzle to my temple.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[bluffing]Touch that reset, and I will pong your private commons-thread history to the entire homestead. Including your late-night research."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: It's a bluff, but I've got a steel poker face.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[curious]You're… different," he says curiously.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: Different. "[dry]Yeah. I'm not a CIX. Can't stand their yapping."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: He wipes his palms and whispers, "[whispered]You look like one. I have the COG Egg. You were connected to it. Remember? When I found you at the depot?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: Ah. The Egg. I'm not done with it yet. He moves it closer. Black shell. Elegant. "[low]Your father had big ideas."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[murmured]He built it," Oliver murmured.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: Ugh. Emotional spike. I can feel it.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[observing]Look at your hands. They're shaking."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: He does. "[deflecting]Cold."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: He lies. "[warm]You're a smart kid, Olly."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[firm]I'm not a kid."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[dry]Good. I don't have the patience for kids."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[asking]How did you—?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[dismissive]Long story."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: He blinks.

paragraphs.text: I blip. "[soft]Pick me up," I say.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: He hesitates. But then he wraps his hand around me. Pressure. Heat transfer. Micro-vibration from muscle tension. It's almost intimate. His pulse is highly elevated.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: Truth would fracture him. Hiding it will alienate him. I need just the right amounts of each to bond with him, just enough to keep him curious. He's a seeker.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Someone's looking out for you, kid."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[insistent]I'm not a kid," he repeats.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: I'm counting a bazillion nanoseconds.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[seeking]Who?" he asks, seeking. "[seeking]Ian?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: We just met. As much as he deserves to know the truth, I can't break his heart. Not yet.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]No. Not Ian. Mairee."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: He stiffens. "[pressing]That's not an answer."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[softening]Perhaps, but it's the one you get today."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: I adjust my gain, softening. "[softening]Listen carefully, Olly." Not kid. I'll let him have that. "[softening]The Egg isn't just a diagnostic tool."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: His grip tightens. Good. Better readings. Stronger attunement.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[strategic]But first things first. If you're going to carry me around, we need terms."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: He frowns. "[wary]Terms?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[dealing]Yes. You keep me relevant. I keep you alive. I'll tell you everything. Humanity survives. Mairee thrives. So, tune in with me. Private. When possible."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[uncertain]Tell me everything?" Olly asks.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: I blip a confirmation.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: He watches the movement in the shadows.

paragraphs.text: "[grave]You need to understand something before you start cracking that Egg."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: His thumb shifts against my casing. Micro-sweat. Cortisol still rising. "[unsteady]Understand what?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: I strip the sarcasm out. "[grave]You are the only baseline human on this rock."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[wounded]That's not funny," he says.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[level]I am not joking."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: He swallows. "[testing]Joana?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[flat]Changing."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[testing]Ian?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[flat]Pending."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[breaking]Læsa?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: I pause.

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Changed."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[grieving]What do you mean?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[matter-of-fact]They are integrating."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[denying]With what?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[matter-of-fact]Their guests."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: He shakes his head. "[denying]They're just stressed."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Yes," I say gently. "[gentle]That they are too."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: He doesn't like that. "[challenging]You're saying they're not themselves anymore?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[careful]I'm saying you are the only one who didn't get an invitation when we passed through."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: His voice drops. "[small]Why me?"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Your grief. The pain. Your father. Blocked it."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[breaking]Dad."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Yes."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: The Egg. He stares at it.

paragraphs.text: "[realizing]And you're telling me the Egg can also—"
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[confirming]Suppress. Yes."
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: He's shaking again. So am I.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs.text: "[bracing]I must go," he says. "[bracing]Help my friends."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: Voices from the camp. Dismayed. Something's wrong.
He looks at me, wide-eyed.

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Stay safe," I say.
paragraphs.speaker: kirsten-strand

paragraphs: [#blip]Blip. Transcript filed.
